title: The choice of amniotic fluid in metabolomics for the monitoring of fetus health - update.
abstract: Introduction: In recent years, several studies have highlighted the promising role of metabolomics in the analysis of amniotic fluid (AF), to describe and characterize the interactions occurring between the mother and the fetus during prenatal development. Among the available biological fluids, AF represents an ideal substrate to provide dynamic information regarding fetal organogenesis and metabolism through pregnancy, since it originates from both maternal and fetal tissues and contains substances derived from placenta, fetal skin, lungs, gastric fluid, and fetal urine. Areas covered: In this paper, we provide an update reporting the most recent results on AF metabolomics in the assessment of feto-maternal health, regarding physiological pregnancies but even fields such as prematurity, bronchopulmonary dysplasia, fetal malformations, chromosomopathies, maternal diseases, placental inflammation or infections, maternal diet or exposure to exogenous substances, according to the literature found on MEDLINE since 2015. Expert opinion: Metabolomics shows a promising role in describing both physiology and disease; the goal would be the identification of biomarkers able to precociously and efficaciously detect pathological conditions, allowing the identification of complicated pregnancy and improving their management. However, this field is under development and its reliability still needs to be clarified, especially through more numerous and accurate studies.
